prompt: Nominal return is 7%. Inflation is 3%. What is the EXACT (not approximate) real return, in percent?
explanation: Fisher: (1 + r_real) = (1.07) / (1.03) = 1.0388. Real ≈ 3.88%. The 4% approximation is close enough at low inflation but drifts at higher levels.

prompt: Nominal return is 25%. Inflation is 20% (think Turkey, Argentina, 1970s US). What is the EXACT real return, in percent?
explanation: (1.25) / (1.20) = 1.0417. Real ≈ 4.17%, not 5%. The approximation breaks at high inflation — the multiplicative form is necessary.

prompt: Which is generally the BETTER signal of corporate health in an inflationary period?
choices: Real revenue growth (volume × real price changes).
explanation: In inflation, headline revenue inflates with prices even if real volume is shrinking. Real revenue strips out the price effect and reveals whether the underlying business is actually growing.

prompt: Look up the most recent BLS CPI release. Note both headline CPI YoY and core CPI YoY.
title: BLS — Consumer Price Index
reflectionPrompt: What's the headline–core gap right now? Which components (food, energy, shelter, services) are driving it?
